meta.title: Farmer Drop — The Books: Six-Ledger Concept Structure
meta.purpose: Spec-layer reconstruction of the backend ledger. Magnus stated (verbally, 2026-07) that the backend runs SIX separate ledgers reconciled via double-entry, built on the basis Volkmar laid (.7 backend). The enumeration below is INFERRED from the documented fee model and marketplace-ledger practice — built to survive being wrong: the chart of ACCOUNTS is primary, the six ledgers are a partition over accounts, and every posting rule is account-level so any regrouping is a re-tag, not a rewrite.
meta.status: six-ledger enumeration ⚠ INFERRED — reconcile against Volkmar's .7 backend (RQ-1)
meta.named_pain: Refunds and partial refunds: unwinding partially-paid taxes and reversing them in Stripe's tax reporting (Magnus, verbal).
in_repo_evidence: TRANSCRIPTS_full.md 00:21:28 — 'the ledger system, the way it has been done, is offering that flexibility to us'
Farmer_Drop__knowledge_map.md §8 — 'Ledger system chosen for flexibility; a .7 backend prevents the expansion-vs-tech bottleneck'
decision_rules_respected: DR-1: take-% UNDECIDED (band 7–12%, centered 10%) — all take-derived amounts parametric, never hardcoded.
DR-2: the 2% credit boost is a cost line with a NAMED funding account (5100), never a silent loss.

ledgers.name: Cash & Stripe Balance
ledgers.evidence: Phase 0 = Stripe rails; T+2 payouts; $0.55 covers Stripe's $0.30 + $0.25 fixed legs; mirrors Stripe balance_transactions.

ledgers.name: Member Credits
ledgers.evidence: The one ledger the knowledge map names explicitly ('fiat rails + credits ledger'); DR-2 demands the 2% boost be a visible funded line.

ledgers.name: Farmer / Supplier Payables
ledgers.evidence: Farmer nets ~80%; released via Connect transfers (0.25% + $0.25 leg); per-supplier subaccounts are how variable suppliers scale.

ledgers.name: Platform Revenue & Fees
ledgers.evidence: The fee engine is a first-class concept node; §3.4.3 requires pass-through vs margin to be separable.

ledgers.name: Tax
ledgers.evidence: Magnus's stated pain — unwinding partially-paid taxes against Stripe Tax reporting — only exists as a pain if tax is its own reconciled ledger.

ledgers.name: Network Commissions & Hops
ledgers.evidence: Contractor 3%-of-drop-volume × N months is a time-based accrual with aging — structurally unlike farmer payables.

H2.label: Alternate — clearing variant
H2.note: L3+L6 merge into one payables book ('everyone we owe'); the sixth ledger is an Orders/Receivables CLEARING book (authorized-not-captured, undelivered orders). Supported by the test-app flow — 'purchases with the Stripe card, no money withdrawn' is exactly a clearing-ledger state. Regrouping = re-tag 2150 under the payables book and promote 1100; zero posting-rule changes.

H3.label: Alternate — rail-split variant
H3.note: L1 splits fiat cash vs token-rail wallet (CPDR / Base), anticipating Phase 1 — per the compadres chain==event==DB==UI reconciliation doctrine already cited in transfer_rails_data.json. Regrouping = split L1 on the rail dimension; zero posting-rule changes.

hypotheses.design_rule: Accounts are the atoms; ledgers are a view. Whichever six Volkmar actually has, reconciliation is a re-grouping exercise, not a rewrite. In the partition view, each book's NET POSITION is its implied due-to/due-from against the other books, and the six nets sum to zero — that identity is the cross-book reconciliation, and the simulator checks it live. Whether Volkmar's six instead balance INTERNALLY via explicit due-to/due-from accounts is part of RQ-1.

accounts.name: Bank – Operating
accounts.purpose: Real settled cash; endpoint of Stripe payouts.

accounts.name: Stripe Balance – Available
accounts.purpose: Mirror of Stripe available balance. All refunds & disputes debit here — refunds always leave the PLATFORM balance (R1).

accounts.name: Stripe Balance – Pending
accounts.purpose: Captures land here pre-settlement; available on a 2-day rolling basis (T+2, US cards).

accounts.name: Rail Wallet – Token/USDC
accounts.purpose: Rail-agnostic settlement endpoint; the canonical 'new rail = new 10xx account' exemplar. Phase-1 CPDR coin posts identical journals here.

accounts.name: Orders Clearing / Suspense
accounts.purpose: Separates 'money arrived' from 'allocated'; entry point when credits are the tender. ⚠ H2 promotes this to the standalone sixth ledger.

accounts.name: Farmer Payout Receivable
accounts.purpose: Clawback claim when a refund/chargeback lands AFTER the farmer was paid (negative-balance case).

accounts.name: Dispute Receivable
accounts.purpose: Contingent asset for disputed funds Stripe has withdrawn but the platform may win back — withdrawn cash, so it lives in the cash book (re-tagged from L4 per challenge C-3).

accounts.name: Member Credits Liability
accounts.purpose: Stored value owed to members incl. the 2% boost. No sales tax at purchase; tax at redemption (R3). ⚠ needs lot sub-tracking: cash-origin vs boost-origin.

accounts.name: Farmer Payable (control)
accounts.purpose: Owed to farmers; per-supplier subaccounts 2100.<farmer_id>.

accounts.name: Distributor / Hop Payable (control)
accounts.purpose: Owed to hops/drivers; subaccounts 2150.<hop_id>.

accounts.name: Contractor Commission Accrual
accounts.purpose: Time-accrued 3%-of-host-drop-volume owed to independent contractors, aged over N months.

accounts.name: Sales Tax Payable (control)
accounts.purpose: Collected tax owed to authorities; subaccounts 2300.<jurisdiction>; ORIGINAL rate stored per order line (R3).

accounts.name: Retained Earnings
accounts.purpose: Period-close bucket for net P&L.

accounts.name: Platform Take Revenue
accounts.purpose: The ~10% take on goods (DR-1 open; parametric).

accounts.name: Fixed Surcharge Revenue
accounts.purpose: The $0.55 (card rail) / $0.25 (credits rail — transfer leg only). Retained on ANY refund, consistently: it covers Stripe's sunk fixed legs, which are sunk on refund too. Making the member whole anyway is a policy choice (RQ-7). A separate account makes the rule structural.

accounts.name: Cart Surcharge Revenue
accounts.purpose: The 3.15% (2.9% Stripe passthrough + 0.25% wedge). Refunded proportionally on partial refund.

accounts.name: Distribution Margin Revenue
accounts.purpose: The RESIDUAL of the goods split (absorbs rounding by construction). Exactly $0 under the canonical T1 Flat-Ten with two hops (80+10+10=100) — it exists for self-haul/one-hop drops and tiered templates, not as a revenue line to count on (challenge C-4).

accounts.name: Credits Breakage Revenue
accounts.purpose: Expired/unspent credits recognized proportional to redemptions (ASC 606).

accounts.name: Refunds & Returns (contra-revenue)
accounts.purpose: Reverses recognized revenue on refunds/lost disputes WITHOUT mutating original rows — append-only journal.

accounts.name: Stripe Processing Fee Expense
accounts.purpose: 2.9% + $0.30 recognized at settlement.

accounts.name: Stripe Refund Fee Loss
accounts.purpose: OPTIONAL net-zero disclosure reclass (5010←5000) attributing already-sunk fees to refund losses. Stripe keeps the ENTIRE original fee on any refund; it is fully expensed at capture and stays in 5000 unless management wants this attribution (challenge C-12). Who ultimately eats it is RQ-7.

accounts.name: Dispute Fee Expense
accounts.purpose: Non-refundable $15, expensed the moment the cardholder initiates.

accounts.name: Credits Boost Expense
accounts.purpose: The NAMED funding source of the 2% bonus ($100 → $102) — DR-2 closed by construction.

accounts.name: Contractor Commission Expense
accounts.purpose: P&L side of the monthly contractor accrual.

accounts.name: Clawback Write-off / Bad Debt
accounts.purpose: Write-off when a clawback receivable (1200) proves unrecoverable — the policy branch after a farmer-paid chargeback loss or refund. The goods-side revenue reversal itself runs through 4900 (challenge C-10).

accounts.name: Tax Bad-Debt Expense
accounts.purpose: Chargeback tax loss. A chargeback is NOT a refund — remitted tax is generally unrecoverable via the returned-merchandise credit; only the bad-debt route, which varies and is limited (e.g. CA) (R3).

reserved_accounts.name: Chargeback / Dispute Reserve
reserved_accounts.note: Provision for expected dispute losses; instantiate when dispute volume justifies provisioning.

reserved_accounts.name: Deferred Revenue (unearned)
reserved_accounts.note: Paid-but-undelivered baskets, if recognition is deferred to fulfillment (book clock).

reserved_accounts.name: Refunds Payable
reserved_accounts.note: Refunds owed but not yet disbursed, if disbursement is async.

partition_tally.note: 20 of 26 are exercised by the demo event set; 1000/1050/1100/4030 are chartered ahead of their flows (bank payout, token rail, clearing-tender variant, breakage sweep); 5010/5300 are the optional-disclosure and write-off branches.

stripe_tax_unwind.recording: Tax.Calculation at checkout → tax.Transaction.create_from_calculation records the taxable transaction (calculations expire after 90 days; reference must be unique across ALL transactions incl. reversals).
stripe_tax_unwind.reversal_api: tax.Transaction reversal with mode=full, or mode=partial two ways: line_items[] each carrying original_line_item + reference + negative amount + negative amount_tax; or flat_amount — a single negative after-tax amount distributed PROPORTIONALLY across line items and shipping by remaining refundable amount. Max 30 partial reversals per sale; over-reversing errors out.
stripe_tax_unwind.reporting_caveat: Reversals do NOT auto-adjust taxable/nontaxable amounts and carry no reversal reason. Stripe RECOMMENDS not refunding partial line-item tax — instead FULLY REVERSE the transaction and CREATE A NEW corrected one for an accurate calculation (docs.stripe.com/tax/reports). This is the exact friction behind the named pain.
stripe_tax_unwind.strategy: Same-period partial refunds: mode=partial (flat_amount for lump-sum, line_items when tied to lines) mirrored 1:1 in the ledger. Cross-period or mixed-taxability refunds: full-reverse-and-recreate, with the ledger booking the amended-return posture (tax clock). Which one Volkmar's code does today is RQ-3.
stripe_tax_unwind.liability_model: Stripe Tax for MARKETPLACES = platform is taxpayer/merchant of record (collects, files, remits). Consistent with marketplace-facilitator law: the platform, not the farmer, is the legal collector in effectively every sales-tax state (R3). Which model is configured is RQ-10.

sources.label: Stripe — refunds & fee non-return
sources.note: Multiple partial refunds up to captured total; original processing fee NOT returned (support.stripe.com/questions/understanding-fees-for-refunded-payments); refunds always from platform balance.

sources.label: Stripe Connect — transfer reversals
sources.note: reverse_transfer proportional on partial refunds; destination account keeps funds by default, platform covers negative; debit_negative_balances (US/CA).

sources.label: Stripe — dispute lifecycle
sources.note: $15 fee on initiation (non-refundable); evidence 7–21d; cardholder window ~120d cards / 180d LPM; funds_withdrawn/funds_reinstated; no refunds while dispute open.

sources.label: Stripe Tax — transaction reversals
sources.note: mode=full|partial; line_items or flat_amount (proportional); 30-reversal cap; cannot over-reverse.

sources.label: Stripe Tax — reports & the partial-reversal caveat
sources.note: Reversals don't adjust taxable amounts or carry reason; Stripe recommends full-reverse-and-recreate; location reports aggregate transactions AND reversals per jurisdiction filing period.

sources.label: Stripe Tax for marketplaces
sources.note: Platform as taxpayer/merchant of record vs Tax-for-platforms (connected account as taxpayer).

sources.label: Stripe — balance transaction taxonomy
sources.note: Canonical type list the mirror ledger maps; transfer reversals surface as transfer_refund; fee_details[] expands into separate postings.

sources.label: Modern Treasury — Accounting for Developers
sources.note: Debits/credits, account normality, marketplace chart-of-accounts pattern; effective_at vs posted_at bitemporal fields.

sources.label: Modern Treasury — enforcing ledger immutability
sources.note: Append-only; discard-and-recreate on pending→posted; never mutate posted rows.

sources.label: TigerBeetle — two-phase transfers
sources.note: Pending/post/void transfers; balanced-by-construction; auth-as-hold with no posted GL impact.

sources.label: Formance — Numscript & programmable ledger
sources.note: Declarative balanced money movement; integer math; deterministic rounding; marketplace fee splits.

sources.label: Stripe engineering — Ledger
sources.note: Immutable log; money movement as observable state machine; double-entry as proof; reconciliation at 5B events/day.

sources.label: Uber — LedgerStore / Gulfstream
sources.note: Immutable ledger, business/event timestamp distinct from system time, sealing.

sources.label: Airbnb — Tracking the Money
sources.note: Event-based subledger generation; 'no money without a source'.

sources.label: Marketplace-facilitator laws (2026)
sources.note: Platform is the legal collector/remitter in effectively every sales-tax state; thresholds vary; aggregate facilitated sales count.

sources.label: Grocery taxability by state (2026)
sources.note: 37 states + DC exempt unprepared groceries; ~13 tax them (full or reduced); prepared-food and candy/soda carve-outs; local divergence.

sources.label: Sales-tax refund mechanics
sources.note: Returned-merchandise deduction in the current period vs amended return; reverse at ORIGINAL rate; statutory windows.

sources.label: Bad-debt route for chargebacks
sources.note: Chargeback tax recovery runs through bad-debt deduction, not returned merchandise; limited in some states (CA).

sources.label: ASC 606 breakage
sources.note: Breakage recognized proportional to actual redemptions.
